subject: ENGLISH
title: Maxine’s Little Diary
objective: Recognize useful features of a simple diary entry.
introduction: A model diary entry may include a date, an event, and the writer’s thoughts or feelings. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Maxine’s Little Diary”.
A key word used in the lesson “Maxine’s Little Diary”.
A key word used in the lesson “Maxine’s Little Diary”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: A model diary entry may include a date, an event, and the writer’s thoughts or feelings.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: A model diary entry may include a date, an event, and the writer’s thoughts or feelings.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.

examples: July 13 — I planted mongo seeds. I felt excited.
Today we read a funny story.
I noticed the first tiny leaf.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

fits: July 13 — I planted mongo seeds. I felt excited.
Today we read a funny story.
I noticed the first tiny leaf.
doesNotFit: A random word with no event

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

options: I felt proud of my plant.

feedback.correct: Correct: I felt proud of my plant.
feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. The pot is blue. and July 13 do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

pairs.left: July 13 — I planted mongo seeds. I felt excited.
Today we read a funny story.
I noticed the first tiny leaf.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Maxine’s Little Diary”?

options.text: A model diary entry may include a date, an event, and the writer’s thoughts or feelings.
A random word with no event

items.explanation: The correct response is “A model diary entry may include a date, an event, and the writer’s thoughts or feelings.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A model diary entry may include a date, an event, and the writer’s thoughts or feelings.

options.text: July 13 — I planted mongo seeds. I felt excited.
A random word with no event

items.explanation: The correct response is “July 13 — I planted mongo seeds. I felt excited.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A model diary entry may include a date, an event, and the writer’s thoughts or feelings.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?

options.text: July 13 — I planted mongo seeds. I felt excited.
Today we read a funny story.

items.explanation: The correct response is “A shopping list only” because it follows the lesson explanation: A model diary entry may include a date, an event, and the writer’s thoughts or feelings.

options.text: I felt proud of my plant.

items.explanation: The correct response is “I felt proud of my plant.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A model diary entry may include a date, an event, and the writer’s thoughts or feelings.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?

options.text: I noticed the first tiny leaf.

items.explanation: The correct response is “I noticed the first tiny leaf.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A model diary entry may include a date, an event, and the writer’s thoughts or feelings.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Maxine’s Little Diary; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Maxine’s Little Diary.
